normalized_title: State Planning Policy 2.4 - Planning for Basic Raw Materials
scope_summary: Ensures basic raw materials (sand, clay, hard rock, limestone, agricultural lime, gravel, gypsum and similar construction materials) and extractive industry matters are considered in planning and development decision-making across Western Australia. It applies to planning instruments, subdivision and development proposals over or adjacent to identified BRM resources, protecting Significant Geological Supply areas and Extraction Sites from encroachment by incompatible (including residential/sensitive) land uses. Exempts Mining Act 1978 matters, State Agreement Act areas and tidal dredging; SPP 2.5 covers BRM on rural land with this policy prevailing on conflict.

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
EPA Guidance Statement No. 3 - Separation Distances between Industrial and Sensitive Land Uses

quality_flags: two-column PDF extraction interleaves columns in places (sentences from adjacent columns merged)
summary.json title is wrong ('Department of Planning,' - publisher line captured instead of the policy title)
separation distances are incorporated by reference to EPA Guidance Statement No. 3 - no numeric distances stated in the policy itself
